qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page provides instructions on how to set up and access Arbutus object storage using the AWS Command Line Interface (CLI).

qa_pairs.question: What is the AWS CLI in the context of Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer: The AWS CLI is one of the available object storage clients for Arbutus object storage.

qa_pairs.question: What are the advantages of using the AWS CLI for Arbutus object storage compared to other clients?
qa_pairs.answer: The AWS CLI offers better support for large files (over 5GB) and includes the helpful `sync` command.

qa_pairs.question: Are all features of the AWS CLI fully tested when used with Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer: No, not all features of the AWS CLI have been tested with Arbutus object storage.

qa_pairs.question: How do I install the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: You can install the AWS CLI by running the command: `pip install awscli awscli-plugin-endpoint`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step for configuring the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: The initial step for configuring the AWS CLI is to generate an access key ID and a secret key.

qa_pairs.question: Which command is used to generate an access key ID and secret key?
qa_pairs.answer: You can generate these credentials by running the command: `openstack ec2 credentials create`.

qa_pairs.question: Where should the generated access key ID and secret key be added?
qa_pairs.answer: The generated credentials should be added to or created in the `~/.aws/credentials` file.

qa_pairs.question: What is the required format for adding credentials to the `~/.aws/credentials` file?
qa_pairs.answer:
The `~/.aws/credentials` file should be formatted as follows:
```
[default]
aws_access_key_id = <access_key>
aws_secret_access_key = <secret_key>
```

qa_pairs.question: What other configuration file needs to be edited for AWS CLI setup?
qa_pairs.answer: The `~/.aws/config` file needs to be edited to add additional configuration.

qa_pairs.question: What configuration settings should be added to the `~/.aws/config` file for Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer:
The following configuration should be added to `~/.aws/config`:
```
[plugins]
endpoint = awscli_plugin_endpoint

[profile default]
s3 =
  endpoint_url = https://object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca
  signature_version = s3v4
s3api =
  endpoint_url = https://object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca
```

qa_pairs.question: What is the endpoint URL for Arbutus object storage when configuring the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: The endpoint URL for Arbutus object storage is `https://object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: Which signature version should be specified for S3 in the AWS CLI configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: Signature version `s3v4` should be specified for S3 in the AWS CLI configuration.

qa_pairs.question: How do I set the AWS profile before executing AWS CLI commands?
qa_pairs.answer: You can set the AWS profile by running the command: `export AWS_PROFILE=default`.

qa_pairs.question: What command is used to list containers with the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: To list containers, you use the command: `aws s3 ls <container-name>`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I synchronize a local directory with an S3 container using the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: You can synchronize a local directory with an S3 container using the command: `aws s3 sync local_directory s3://container-name/prefix`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more examples of using the AWS CLI?
qa_pairs.answer: More examples of using the AWS CLI can be found on this external site: `https://docs.ovh.com/us/en/storage/getting_started_with_the_swift_S3_API/`.
